title: Inventory clean cooking and health data sources for Sub-Saharan Africa
outcome: A dated source inventory separating household surveys, health data, access data, enterprise data, and unusable sources.
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic grain, and limitations.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons.

title: Estimate sub-national household air pollution health burden
outcome: A reproducible baseline of household air pollution-attributable disease burden at the finest sub-national grain that available data permits.
inputs: Global Burden of Disease HAP layer
expected_artifact: validation.md update with health burden estimation methodology and data checks.
validation_method: Replicator reruns burden estimation on independent survey data subset and confirms estimates within reported uncertainty.
done_condition: Burden estimation includes uncertainty bounds, sub-national grain documentation, and modeling assumptions with replication confirmed.

title: Classify and map clean cooking adoption barriers
outcome: A spatial classification of adoption barriers categorized as economic, infrastructural, cultural, and regulatory with cited evidence for each barrier class.
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with barrier classification and spatial analysis.
validation_method: Replicator confirms that each barrier classification is traceable to a cited source and that barrier classes are well-defined.
done_condition: Barrier classification includes economic, infrastructural, cultural, and regulatory categories with spatial extent mapped and replication confirmed.

title: Model health benefits under different adoption scenarios
outcome: A scenario analysis estimating health benefits under different clean cooking adoption rates, explicitly modeling fuel stacking and imperfect adoption.
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with scenario analysis results.
validation_method: Replicator reruns scenario analysis with alternative fuel-stacking assumptions and confirms benefit ranges.
done_condition: Scenario analysis includes at least three adoption scenarios, explicitly models fuel stacking, and includes caveat that modeled benefits assume sustained use.

title: Red-team overstated health benefit and adoption claims
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying where health benefit estimates could be overstated, adoption barriers mischaracterized, or technology transition recommendations could cause harm.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every high-risk failure mode has a mitigation or blocks publication, then a second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every health-benefit or adoption claim is either qualified with fuel-stacking caveats, softened to analytic language, or gated behind replication.

title: Check whether outputs match clean cooking program decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether burden maps and barrier analyses would change any real clean cooking program decision responsibly.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk for the output, then a second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user, named decision, explicit fuel-stacking caveats, and replicated field-reality review before publication.
